steps.title: Spot the structure inside each list item
steps.content: The sentence lists multiple measurement units, each with the same internal structure: a name followed by a comma and then a description (for example, “the sa, a unit of area” and “the khar, a unit of volume”). This means each list item already contains a comma.

steps.title: Use semicolons to separate items containing commas
steps.content:
When list items contain internal commas, use semicolons (not commas) to separate the items.

Choice C applies this rule correctly: “area; the shesep, a unit of length;” creates the pattern the sa, a unit of area; the shesep, a unit of length; and the khar, a unit of volume—semicolons between items, commas within them.

Choices A, B, and D fail by splitting names from descriptions, omitting separators, or mixing punctuation types.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
